MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Brian McDonald for and with (petitioner) Fatima Martinez.

The Court stated that it would like to walk through the issues starting with the trustee not paying rent. Mr. McDonald reported that this is the family home, that the minor also lives there, that the beneficiaries want to keep the home and that it would be costly if they had to move. Mr. McDonald reported on the depletion of assets stating that they put together a schedule, provided a copy to the court and explained how they came up with it, that the trust will not run out of funds and that in 5 years the oldest beneficiary will be 25 and due his distribution. Mr. McDonald reported on the issue of Brett's rent, that he has an apartment at St. Mary's for $1,000.00 per month, that they cannot rent it for only 9 months, that Brett is there on a baseball scholarship and is required to be there during the summer, that the petitioner pays for his food and gas and has not requested reimbursement from the court, that the 1% fee guideline is appropriate, that the funds are in a blocked account, that the examiner has requested that they request funds for rent every three months, that in regards to fees, the first accounting was confusing and they spent a lot of time on it, that much less time was spent on the second accounting and that they anticipate the same going forward. The accounting petition was taken under submission.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Kabasares. (Not Reported)

Off Calendar Jun-04-2012 Continued To Jun-25-12 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Richard Canatella for and with Fatima Martinez Rasso (Petitioner). Nancy Rasch Guardian Ad Litem for Minor Beneficiary. Tiffany Rasso also present.

Ms. Rasch stated that she would like to see a neutral Private Fiduciary appointed as Successor Trustee, that there are too many conflicts in this case with regard to representation of Counsel Richard Canatella with many of the Family members at different times and that the house is underwater. In addition, Ms. Rasch stated that the house was transferred to the Trust, that no one seems to know how the mortgage is being paid, that there is an automatic debit from the Trust, that these assets are now the Minor's assets and that they need an accounting from Mr. Canatella. Furthermore, Ms. Rasch stated that repairs are being made to the house in South San Francisco, that the remodeling goes far beyond the scope (Wine Cellar), that there are too many unanswered questions, that Louie Rasso is now deceased (5/5/12) and that Mr. Canatella's Power of Attorney died with Mr. Rasso. The Petition to Fill Vacancy in Trusteeship, etc. was continued to June 25, 2012 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for Ms. Rasch to file something that spells out her objections to the petition. Objections are to be filed no later than June 11, 2012. Responses are to be filed no later than June 20, 2012.
